summary: Corneria stage decomp/matching PR continuing from #2381. It primarily expands and cleans up src/melee/gr/grcorneria.c, adds/adjusts GroundVars typing in src/melee/gr/types.h, and reports 8 newly matched grcorneria items plus 10 improved unmatched items, with one .sdata regression noted by the bot.

key_files.role: Main Corneria stage implementation; large matching update for arwing spawning, arwing/corneria callbacks, animation, object lookup, control flow, and typed field access.
GroundVars header update; adjusts Corneria and Arwing field types, adds grCorneria_GroundVars2, and adds the corneria2 union arm.

what_changed.text: Added includes for it/items/itarwinglaser.h and pl/player.h in grcorneria.c.

Adjusted local Corneria data structs in grcorneria.c: several grCn_StageData fields changed from s32 to f32, grCn_Data x3D8 changed from a 9x3 matrix to a flat 27-element array, and x478 became an s32[3] with smaller padding.
Added or substantially filled grCorneria_801DCE1C, including arwing slot counting, timer decrement/reset behavior, far/near type selection, unique type picking, and spawn calls.
evidence_refs: text_corpus bot report: grCorneria_801DCE1C improved 0.00% to 80.85%

what_changed.text: Converted several generated-looking branches into switch/case or clearer conditional forms, including stage selection in grCorneria_801DCCFC and arwing type dispatch in grCorneria_801DE8E4.
evidence_refs: text_corpus bot report: grCorneria_801DCCFC and grCorneria_801DE8E4 reached 100.00%

what_changed.text: Replaced raw pointer arithmetic and casts with typed field access in multiple grcorneria functions, including use of grCn_Data* data, gp->gv.arwing.xE0 as Vec3, and gp->gv.corneria2 fields.
Changed grArwing_GroundVars fields xE0/xE4/xE8 from separate f32 members to Vec3 xE0, matching later code that passes &gp->gv.arwing.xE0 to HSD_JObjSetTranslate.

Added grCorneria_GroundVars2 to types.h for an alternate Corneria ground-vars union interpretation, including HSD_GObj pointer fields and integer state/timer fields, then added the corneria2 union arm to Ground.
Cleaned dialog joint setup in grCorneria_801E25C4 by using struct grSmashTaunt_GroundVars fields and a for loop over dialog_joints instead of byte-offset writes and pointer-table stepping.

Bot report recorded 8 new matches: grCorneria_801DE8E4, grCorneria_801DF8D0, grCorneria_801DDE88, grCorneria_801E08CC, grCorneria_801E1970, grCorneria_801E1060, grCorneria_801DCCFC, and grCorneria_801E2228.

Bot report recorded 10 improvements in unmatched items, including grCorneria_801DED50, grCorneria_801DCE1C, grCorneria_801E1348, grCorneria_801DE024, grCorneria_801E03C8, .sdata2, grCorneria_801DDAC4, grCorneria_801E1BF0, grCorneria_801E25C4, and grCorneria_801DE568.
Bot report also noted one unmatched-item regression: main/melee/gr/grcorneria .sdata dropped from 78.79% to 72.90%.

smart_moves.text: Introduced a dedicated grCorneria_GroundVars2 union arm rather than continuing to write through grCorneria_GroundVars float fields or raw offsets for arwing-slot callbacks.
Flattened suspicious table access and then used direct indexed access where the code accesses x3D8 as a single sequence, reducing artificial multi-dimensional indexing.
Used project/common macros and constants such as ABS and M_TAU in rotation/angle cleanup instead of hand-written sign checks and 2.0 * M_PI expressions.
Kept verification feedback visible: the bot report shows substantial text progress while also surfacing a .sdata regression, which should not be ignored in curation.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When the same ground-vars union storage is used by callbacks with a different interpretation, a dedicated union arm can be cleaner and safer than reusing unrelated fields or raw byte offsets.
Converting separate x/y/z float fields into a Vec3 can unlock natural API calls such as HSD_JObjSetTranslate(jobj, &vec) and remove casts to Vec3*.
A natural for loop over a fixed table can replace generated do-while pointer stepping when the table index is semantically meaningful and typed fields are available.
Grouped switch cases are useful for compactly representing stage IDs or type ranges when the diff shows equivalent branch ladders being replaced and matching improves.
evidence_refs: text_corpus bot report: both items reached 100.00%

naming_conventions.text: The PR uses a short local pointer name data for &grCn_803E1D38 when many accesses target the same stage data singleton.
New alternate ground-vars type is named grCorneria_GroundVars2 and accessed as gp->gv.corneria2, reflecting an additional union interpretation rather than renaming the existing grCorneria_GroundVars.
Helper names retained the local grCn_ prefix for static inline helpers such as grCn_RandRange, grCn_CheckFar, grCn_SpawnArwing, and grCn_PickUniqueType.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK adjustments were used narrowly in grCorneria_801DE024 and grCorneria_801E25C4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The PR slice has bot score evidence but no inline reviewer explanation for why each PAD_STACK is required; keep as tactic evidence, not a broad style rule.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Declaration placement and local lifetimes were adjusted, such as moving Vec3 translate earlier in grCorneria_801DE024 and using local temporaries for switch/rotation code.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Treat as function-local matching work; no global convention is proven.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Direct user data access appears in selected places, e.g. Ground* gp = gobj->user_data, while other sites use GET_GROUND or HSD_GObjGetUserData.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The slice does not explain why direct access was needed; do not promote this as a preferred idiom without additional local evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Macro substitutions ABS and M_TAU replaced generated sign checks and 2.0 * M_PI in angle normalization.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Supported for this function by the diff and bot improvement, but no exact objdiff trace is included beyond aggregate bot report.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Using __FILE__ in the OSReport call replaced a hard-coded "grcorneria.c" string.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: No review discussion explains the motivation; keep as local matching observation.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Dedicated union arms beat raw offsets for alternate GroundVars interpretations
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2383 added grCorneria_GroundVars2 and the corneria2 union arm, then used gp->gv.corneria2 fields in grcorneria.c instead of reusing unrelated grCorneria_GroundVars fields or byte-offset writes for arwing-slot callback state.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Vec3 recovery can remove casts and expose authored API usage
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2383 changed grArwing_GroundVars xE0/xE4/xE8 into Vec3 xE0 and updated grcorneria code to initialize x/y/z and pass &gp->gv.arwing.xE0 to HSD_JObjSetTranslate.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Control-flow cleanup coincided with verified grcorneria matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: In this PR, switch/case cleanup for stage/type dispatch appears in functions that the bot report lists as reaching 100%, including grCorneria_801DCCFC and grCorneria_801DE8E4.

source_update_candidates.title: Corneria arwing ground-var typing spans grcorneria.c and gr/types.h
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2383 shows that Corneria arwing callback decomp work may require paired edits in src/melee/gr/grcorneria.c and src/melee/gr/types.h, including Ground.gv union-arm additions and grArwing_GroundVars field typing.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially useful path-scoped fact for future Corneria/arwing work; should be reviewed before adding to source-owned path facts.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not treat PAD_STACK in this PR as broad style without exact evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2383 contains narrow PAD_STACK uses while the loaded evidence provides only aggregate bot score output, not exact per-function objdiff explanations. This supports reinforcing that PAD_STACK remains a tactic requiring narrow verification rather than a general cleanup idiom.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is consistent with the loaded matching-tactics standard, but the curator should decide whether the PR adds anything beyond the existing standard.

source_update_candidates.title: Possible Corneria arwing data layout notes
source_update_candidates.text: The PR changes local grCn_Data field interpretations: x3D8 becomes s32[27], x478 becomes s32[3], positions remains Vec3[14], and anim_ids remains s32[14]. These may be useful for data-sheet review if corroborated by current source/symbol evidence.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR evidence supports that these local declarations changed, but data ownership/layout facts should be verified against current source and symbol metadata before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that the .sdata regression was accepted intentionally; the slice only shows the bot reported it and that the PR merged.
Do not infer reviewer rationale; no human review comments are loaded.
Do not promote direct gobj->user_data access as a global convention from this PR alone.
Do not treat grCorneria_GroundVars2 field names like xE0/xE4/xF8 as semantic names; they are still offset-style placeholders in the evidence.
Do not claim every changed function matched; the bot distinguishes 8 new matches from 10 unmatched-item improvements.
